name: Brute Ratel C4 / Havoc Framework
categoryDescription: Commonly abused tools that leave NO Windows registry artifacts. These tools are typically single executables, portable builds, or in-memory frameworks. Detection depends entirely on non-registry evidence: process execution logs, prefetch, ShimCache/Amcache, BAM/DAM, network traffic, and memory forensics. Includes detection notes for each tool.
description: Commercial (Brute Ratel) and open-source (Havoc) post-exploitation frameworks designed to evade EDR. Badgers/demons run in memory. No native registry entries unless persistence is configured.
forensic_value: No registry artifacts. Brute Ratel specifically designed to evade common EDR signatures. Focus on behavioral indicators: ETW tampering, unusual memory allocations in legitimate processes.
